# TODO

PATH CHECKFOR SINGLE typst file

## Rendering

- [ ] Split CSS into core and style
- [ ] Better CSS
- [ ] Accessibility
    - [ ] html + css
    - [ ] typst
    - [ ] latex
    - [ ] pdf
- [ ] *Calepin* always in italics with capital letter in website/
- [ ] Language / localization (`lang:` option, custom translation strings)
- [ ] Allow users to supply `postprocess.latex` or `postprocess.html` in `_calepin/`
- [ ] How do lettered appendices work?


## Code execution

- [ ] Code annotation (line markers `# <1>` with numbered explanations)
- [ ] Multiple figures from a single code chunk with `fig-subcap`
- [ ] Column option for code output placement (`column: margin`, `column: page`, etc.)
- [ ] Chunk labels should not add a fig- or a tbl- automatically if that is already in the label specified in ```{lang, fig-blahblah}. Otherwise we get duplicated fig-fig-blahblah.
- [ ] Render websites in parallel limit ncpu


## Tables

- [ ] How can an R and Python chunks add content to the LaTeX preamble?
- [ ] How can I make the full TOML available to R and PYthon?
- [ ] Tables should use a template and filter
- [ ] Advanced table features (Bootstrap classes, embedded markdown in cells)
- [ ] Subtables
- [ ] Table caption location: does this work in all formats?


## Philosophy

- [ ] High performance
- [ ] Simplicity
    - [ ] Classless HTML with simple CSS.
    - [ ] Minimal preambles in LaTeX and Typst, with optional variants.
- [ ] Everything is local. No global config.
- [ ] Templates everywhere
- [ ] Limited compatibility layer with Quarto
- [ ] Robust configuration in TOML
- [ ] Experimental stage to allow experimentation and breaking changes in the first stages of dev.


## Math

- [ ] Typst math is currently suppressed
- [ ] LaTeX to Typst math plugin


## Typst

- [ ] ship with typst-lib in rust since this is our default -t pdf engine
- [ ] book template


## Figures

- [ ] Figure templates in latex are pre-computed. Templates need to handle \begin{ {{variables}} }
- [ ] Check insertion of static images
- [ ] Match default DPI in python and R. Or does it matter for .svg files? Are we doing svg in python?
- [ ] Can we convert svg2pdf without calling an external tool?
- [ ] Auto-scaling of figures should not be disabled when fig-width is set in the .toml file. Only when set on a per-figure basis.
- [ ] Check if #| fig-align works in all base formats.

## Layout

- [ ] Column classes (`.column-body`, `.column-page`, `.column-screen`, `.column-margin`)
- [ ] Outset variants (`.column-body-outset`, `.column-page-inset`)
- [ ] Left/right overflow (`.column-screen-left`, `.column-page-right`, etc.)
- [ ] Margin content (`.column-margin` for text, figures, tables)
- [ ] Margin references (`reference-location: margin`)
- [ ] Margin citations (`citation-location: margin`)
- [ ] Margin captions (`cap-location: margin`, `fig-cap-location: margin`)
- [ ] PDF page geometry options (`geometry:` front matter)
- [ ] Screen inset shaded (`column: screen-inset-shaded`)


## Cross-references

- [ ] Custom cross-reference types.
- [ ] Custom numbered float types.
- [ ] Cross-references across pages in website and book collections.


## HTML

- [ ] Do not base64 in website/ but always do it in html/
- [ ] Embed resources
- [ ] responsive html: Previous / Next buttons at the bottom of the page get a line return when the page narrows isntead of getting closer to each other.


## calepin new

- [ ] Built-in website template
- [ ] Built-in minimal website template
- [ ] Built-in blog template
- [ ] --variant


## Evangelism

- [ ] arelbundock.com
- [ ] countrycode
- [ ] tinytable
- [ ] modelsummary
- [ ] marginaleffects


## Plugins

- [ ] Stable template API is too large: exposing too many template variables as public API locks in internal rendering details
- [ ] How to register new plugins? What are they allowed to do?
- [ ] ANSI colors in txtfmt plugin
- [ ] CSL helpers: https://github.com/citation-style-language/styles/
- [ ] Test lightbox


## Quarto compatibility

- [ ] Decide on a minimal set of supported Quarto preamble features
- [ ] YAML to TOML translation layer


## TOML

- [ ] Collect all hard-coded values from the rust code
- [ ] Transfer everythin we can to .toml
- [ ] Formal categories: content, content_standalone, what else?


## File paths

- [ ] Strict, consistent, simple, cross-platform
- [ ] Clear separation of output and input locations
- [ ] Format-specific path handling (Typst, LaTeX, HTML, Markdown)
- [ ] Tectonic path resolution quirks
- [ ] _calepin/ directory with cache, files, and output subdirectories?
- [ ] Ask Claude to list all write locations
- [ ] Brainstorm on trade-offs for defaults: intuitive defaults, not too much clutter, not too deeply nested.


## calepin new

- [ ] site
    - [ ] website (calepin)
    - [ ] simple (arelbundock.com)
    - [ ] single (litedown)
    - [ ] blog
- [ ] ebook
    - [ ] book
- [ ] document
- [ ] book


## Other formats

- [ ] docx should be more powerful and maybe not go through pandoc
- [ ] ebook?


## Markdown

- [ ] footnotes should have hover in HTML


## Docs

- [ ] HTML sales pitch: classless CSS, standalone files by default
- [ ] Performance page with `bench/rubbish.sh`
- [ ] Accessibility: 
    - [x] HTML: aria codes, skip to content, etc.
    - [ ] PDF: ???
    - [ ] Automatic alt-text for generated figures?


## Internal

- [ ] document vs. collection (better than "site")
- [ ] Performance profiling
- [ ] Architecture refactor / DRY / simplification
- [ ] Singular vs Plural file names
- [ ] User input validation. Brainstorm with AI to be super strict and informative. Paths, formats, front matter options, etc.
- [ ] run_pagefind() should be specified as a more generic post-processing step that can be specified by the user.
- [ ] cargo-dist
    - [ ] homebrew
    - [ ] linux
    - [ ] windows
- [ ] all variables should be underscore, never dash


## Preview

- [ ] website watch doesn't work in preview mode


## Manual code review

- [ ] `src/filters/`
- [ ] `src/render/`
- [ ] `src/formats/`
- [ ] `src/structures/`
- [ ] `src/parse/`
- [ ] `src/` (root modules: main, types, plugins, cli, util, website, site_builder, preview, compile)
